title: Failed Stage Retry Budget Control
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: failed stage retry budget control.
assumptions: Retry budget is represented as discrete tokens (retry_token) that are exhaustible and must be claimed before a retry attempt.
Executor reservations are required to ensure capacity for a retry and must be released when no longer needed.
Automated checks (test_suite, security_check_profile) are idempotent and produce deterministic artifacts (vulnerability_report, failure_signature) for policy evaluation.
Human reviewers and team approvals are available within the governance window and are modeled as explicit reviewer entities.
Retry policy objects are bound explicitly to runs before retries are permitted; policy binding is immutable for the duration of the decision sequence.
risks: Token starvation: insufficient retry_token units may block legitimate retry attempts under transient failures, causing higher manual intervention.
Race conditions: concurrent claims for the same retry_token or executor_reservation could lead to inconsistent state if not serialized by an external coordinator.
Stale policy binding: if retry_policy instances are updated elsewhere, runs bound to older policy versions may behave unexpectedly without explicit policy versioning.
Approval bottlenecks: reliance on human_reviewer entities can create latency and failure to meet SLAs if oncall/review teams are unavailable.
Resource leakage: unreleased executor_reservation or reserved test_suite assignments can reduce capacity and degrade throughput.
